title: 20 Board Limit on Dashboards MUST Increase
excerpt:
I still can’t believe there is a 20 board limit that can be pulled into dashboards (although we have a little more after speaking to Monday). 
We create new boards for each client so all project comms and tasks and clien…

posts.author: ChrisTMA (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml:
I still can’t believe there is a 20 board limit that can be pulled into dashboards (although we have a little more after speaking to Monday).

We create new boards for each client so all project comms and tasks and client specific (Combining clients into the same board simply does not work). We have a dashboard to show our scheduling workload across all team members, each who touch every client at some point, and we can only pull in a limited number of boards into the dashboard which makes reporting pointless as it is inaccurate as it will not pull in all boards. My team and having to use spreadsheets instead, which doubles up work and makes using Monday a little redundant. This must increase if Monday wishes to stay competitive as other platforms, like ClickUp, have no limit… although I am not a fan of their system. I don’t want to move from Monday but it is becoming an issue.

I can’t be the only one who has reported this?

Please update this feature.

posts.author: Irlene (Irlene)
posts.cookedHtml: i am having this same issue as well and see that this actually goes back as far as 2020 with little progress

posts.author: zakhall (zak hall)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! I am having the same frustration and losing my team’s faith in Monday because of all the workarounds.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you have 10, 20, 100 or even more projects I would consolidate these projects to one board and do the workload and other dashboard functions from that board.

That also eliminates reconfiguring your dashboard widgets when new projects are started.

The Rollup Multiple Boards app creates project boards from templates and aggregate all kind of colum to give a full overview of a project per item.

posts.author: parisvega (Paris Vega)
posts.cookedHtml: @basdebruin Can you give an example of how this works by linking somewhere or sharing a screenshot? I’m trying to wrap my head around how we would structure a single board to handle every project.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @parisvega

A good start can be found here 20|Rollup Multiple Boards - Excellent Team. This app aggregates all kind of data into one board (e.g. %Done, People assigned, Sum of number, Average etc.). This single master board can than be used to feed into a dashboard. The linked page contains a detail document how the app works and a “Try me” link.

posts.author: pkatariya (punam)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @ChrisTMA ,

I just finished a project where client had more than 1500+ boards and wanted to consolidate data to pull visualization and send it to their clients. This was not possible within Monday.

So, We used Monday.com API to pull all the data from each board, consolidate them together and save it in a database following which created a dashboard using other visualization tool.

Now the process is automated and client has access to their data also.

posts.author: nationwidehistology (Heather Marlatt)
posts.cookedHtml: I REALLY need this limit to increase as well. The way we use this software is that each client has a board they are subscribed to so they can see their projects in their board. Then we view all of them at once. I have a lot more than 20 clients so we are having to try to add/remove boards to the dashboard and do dashboards alphabetically. Its a huge pain. I just want to be able to add at least 100 boards to a dashboard if not unlimited.

posts.author: Simon_Loge (Simon Logé)
posts.cookedHtml: can we connect? my email is simonloge@icloud.com would love to hear how you did this!!

posts.author: michelle.scribner (Michelle Scribner)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any progress towards increasing the board limit? This is still a problem for our team

posts.author: ChrisTMA (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml: Afraid not. As far as I am aware its still the same.

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml:
@nationwidehistology @Simon_Loge @michelle.scribner @ChrisTMA
If an add-on is an option, I suggest taking a look at the Analytics & Reports by Screenful . You can use it to create custom charts from any number of Monday boards and compile them into Reports . The reports can then be shared with anyone in your organization via email or Slack as a PDF on a custom schedule. Here’s what it looks like:

You can configure everything once, and the recipients start receiving weekly project status reports that are automatically generated with the most up-to-date data. We have users with 1000+ boards using our product for their reporting needs.

You can learn more about this add-on, or start a free trial to give it a try with your own data.

posts.author: shayan.moussawi (Shayan Moussawi)
posts.cookedHtml:
It is on the roadmap for Monday DB 2.0 -

though I believe it also might have already been implemented- would have to be tested out

posts.cookedHtml:
@nationwidehistology @Simon_Loge @michelle.scribner @ChrisTMA Softr is a no-code app builder that integrates with monday.com, and could be a potential solution for you. It allows you to display data from multiple monday.com boards in one secure app, without board limits.

posts.author: Chfraym (Christian)
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah, I NEED this! My company is trying to move to another platform and I’m tapdancing meeting our needs. Data Visualization is the biggest need in keeping everything organized
